headline: Attackers turned an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day into mass data theft across hundreds of organizations as Washington forced Anthropic to pull its top AI models offline worldwide.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Crossfire
emerging_trends.text: Governments and companies now treat frontier AI as both a controlled weapon and a criminal tool, from Washington pulling Anthropic's newest models to Google suing a Gemini-powered phishing network.

emerging_trends.subject: Decade-Long Hides
emerging_trends.text: Two flaws surfaced today that lurked roughly ten years—Velvet Ant's Linux login backdoor and a phpBB authentication bypass—showing how long deep access goes unnoticed.

emerging_trends.subject: Breach Accountability
emerging_trends.text: Regulators and courts keep attaching record costs to data breaches, with Coupang's 409 million dollar fine and a 47 million dollar 23andMe settlement landing the same day.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply-Chain Strikes
emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep poisoning the software pipeline itself, hijacking more than 400 Arch Linux AUR packages to seed infostealers and an eBPF rootkit.

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day Mass Data Theft
topics.last_24h: ShinyHunters exploited a zero-day in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP software to steal gigabytes of data from hundreds of organizations, hitting American universities hardest.
topics.summary: A critical flaw in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP software let ShinyHunters siphon gigabytes of records from hundreds of organizations. American universities absorbed the brunt because so many run the software for student and HR data. Oracle customers should apply emergency patches and hunt for signs of exfiltration. The breadth and near-maximum severity make this among the day's most damaging exposures.

sources.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data

topics.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise RCE Flaw
topics.last_24h: Splunk patched CVE-2026-20253, a 9.8-severity flaw that lets an unauthenticated attacker create or truncate arbitrary files and reach remote code execution.
topics.summary: Splunk released updates for CVE-2026-20253, rated 9.8 on the CVSS scale. In Splunk Enterprise versions below 10.2.4 and 10.0.7, an unauthenticated attacker can create or truncate arbitrary files and chain that into remote code execution. The lack of any authentication requirement puts exposed deployments at immediate risk. Administrators should upgrade without delay.

sources.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication

topics.title: US Forces Anthropic to Pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5
topics.last_24h: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to block all foreign nationals from Fable 5 and Mythos 5, and the company took both models offline worldwide at 5:21 p.m. ET Friday.
topics.summary: New export controls from the Trump administration directed Anthropic to deny its most advanced models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, to any foreign national inside or outside the country, citing national security. Anthropic complied by disabling both models globally. The company disputes the basis, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the underlying capability common elsewhere. The order marks an aggressive extension of export law to frontier AI.

sources.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls

topics.title: Section 702 Surveillance Authority Lapses
topics.last_24h: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is poised to lapse for the first time since 2008 after a legislative deadlock.
topics.summary: Section 702 lets US intelligence agencies collect the communications of foreign targets abroad, and critics fault it for sweeping up Americans' data in the process. Lawmakers failed to reauthorize the program, so the authority lapses for the first time since Congress passed it in 2008. The gap could disrupt intelligence collection while the debate over privacy safeguards continues.

sources.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock

topics.title: Velvet Ant Backdoors Linux Login for a Decade
topics.last_24h: Sygnia revealed that China-linked Velvet Ant backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components of an isolated network's Linux login system and watched administrator activity for nearly ten years.
topics.summary: The China-nexus group Velvet Ant planted itself inside the Linux authentication stack, tampering with PAM and OpenSSH to control who could sign in and to survive routine cleanup. Sygnia says the access gave the group full visibility into administrative activity on an air-gapped network for close to a decade. The case shows how authentication infrastructure makes durable, low-noise ground for espionage. Defenders should audit PAM modules and SSH binaries for unauthorized modification.

sources.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: Arch Linux AUR Supply-Chain Compromise
topics.last_24h: Attackers hijacked more than 400 Arch User Repository packages, rewriting build scripts to drop a Rust infostealer and, with root, an eBPF rootkit.
topics.summary: The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection. Anyone who built one of the more than 400 tainted packages risked handing over developer secrets, credentials, and access tokens to a Rust binary. When it runs as root, the malware loads an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. Arch users should review recently built AUR packages and rotate exposed secrets.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer

topics.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Over Gemini Abuse
topics.last_24h: Google filed suit against a Chinese cybercrime network it accuses of weaponizing its Gemini AI agent to mass-produce phishing texts aimed at Americans.
topics.summary: Google says the network built and runs a phishing-as-a-service kit called Outsider and used the Gemini AI agent to help craft smishing messages targeting US consumers. The suit seeks to dismantle the operation. The action signals tech firms turning to the courts as criminals fold generative AI into scam pipelines.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing

topics.title: Record Breach Penalties for Coupang and 23andMe
topics.last_24h: South Korea fined Coupang 409 million dollars over a data breach, its largest privacy penalty ever, while a US bankruptcy administrator approved a 47 million dollar settlement for 23andMe breach victims.
topics.summary: South Korea's privacy commission hit e-commerce giant Coupang with a record 409 million dollar fine, eclipsing the 88.8 million dollar penalty it levied against SK Telecom earlier this year. Separately, a US bankruptcy administrator approved a 47 million dollar fund for roughly 7 million 23andMe customers whose genetic data hackers stole starting in April 2023 and posted to the dark web. Together the actions attach steeper financial costs to breaches.

sources.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims

sports: Marlins beat the Pirates 8-3 for their 6th straight

Marlins try to extend road win streak in matchup against
2026 MLB ABS challenge system tracker: Team, player

business_politics.text: The United States and Iran moved to the edge of a deal ending their war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz, with Pakistan's prime minister saying signing could come within 24 hours; oil futures settled at multimonth lows on the prospect. An accord would only open the harder phase of talks over Tehran's nuclear program.

sources.title: Pakistan says US-Iran deal to be signed within 24 hours
Deal With Iran Is Close, but Accord Will Kickstart Toughest Phase of Talks
Oil Futures Fall on Expectations of U.S.-Iran Agreement

business_politics.text: SpaceX raised $75 billion in the largest initial public offering on record, and shares jumped nearly a fifth on debut to value the company at $2.1 trillion, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. Investors bought a money-losing rocket and AI venture under Musk's full control, a marker of how far risk appetite has stretched.

sources.title: SpaceX's surge on debut makes Musk world's first trillionaire
SpaceX, Now Worth $2.1 Trillion, Pulls Off Goldilocks Debut

business_politics.text: U.S. headline annual inflation reached 4.2%, the highest since April 2023, sharpening the case against a wait-and-see central bank as price pressures broaden.

sources.title: Chart of the Week: US headline inflation matters to consumers

business.text: Allegheny County extended the public comment period on a proposal requiring every county business to provide 18 weeks of paid parental leave; supporters call it overdue, while some employers warn it could drive companies out.

sources.title: Allegheny County extends public comment period for paid parental leave proposal

business.text: Manor residents Kelly and Jesse Dowdrick launched WanderBrew, a mobile coffee shop built into a 1978 trailer Kelly found on Facebook Marketplace 32 minutes after pitching the idea.

sources.title: Manor couple launches WanderBrew coffee trailer

business.text: Polymath Park in Westmoreland County opened a row of cedar treehouse-style dining pods tucked among the trees, selling diners seclusion as an increasingly rare amenity.

sources.title: Treehouse-inspired pods offer nature dining in Polymath Park

around_town.text: The $6.8 million Stewart Avenue stormwater project will cut localized flooding and upgrade drainage in two Pittsburgh neighborhoods.

sources.title: $6.8M project aims to reduce flooding in 2 Pittsburgh neighborhoods

around_town.text: Harrisburg leaders say they expect to pass Pennsylvania's budget on time, avoiding a repeat of last year's 135-day impasse that delayed billions of dollars to schools, counties, and nonprofits.

sources.title: Pa. lawmakers optimistic about passing budget on time as deadline looms

around_town.text: State troopers issued nearly 700 citations in the first week of enforcing Paul Miller's Law, Pennsylvania's ban on holding a phone while driving.

sources.title: During first week of Paul Miller's Law enforcement, troopers issue nearly 700 citations

around_town.text: A stretch of the Great Allegheny Passage in West Mifflin closes the evening of July 9 for rock-scaling work to address rockfall hazards.

sources.title: Part of the Great Allegheny Passage is closing while contractors address rockfall hazards

around_town.text: Forecasters warn of strong to severe thunderstorms across the Pittsburgh area Sunday afternoon and evening after 2 p.m., following a sunny, low-humidity Saturday.

sources.title: Strong storms and severe weather could move through the Pittsburgh area on Sunday

events.text: The Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival, honoring the Jeep's Butler County birthplace, runs this weekend at Coopers Lake Campground and along Butler's Main Street, drawing thousands of enthusiasts.

sources.title: Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival marks Jeep's birthplace in Butler, Pa.

events.text: Westmoreland County community events this weekend include a Flag Day observance, an art show, and a fishing derby.

sources.title: Westmoreland happenings: Flag Day observance, art show, fishing derby, more

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is ending, pointing to OpenAI and Anthropic both filing to go public as a scramble for exit liquidity by companies burning billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Thompson examines Fable 5 as the public version of Mythos, finding it capable but warning that its handling of Anthropic alignment and AI tiers sets troubling new precedents.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn't AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport questions the industry's comparison of AI to industrial automation, probing why the technology has yet to eliminate the knowledge jobs its makers predicted it would.
